diagnosis: Sinus. This is another case of pseudo-flutter waves due to artifact (probably Parkinsonian tremor). Some leads show clear sinus P wave, e.g. V2, V3.

report.intervals: PR: ~160 ms, QRS: ~80 ms, QTc: ~430 ms
report.findings: Significant baseline artifact resembling atrial flutter (pseudo-flutter), attributed to Parkinsonian tremor. Distinct sinus P waves are visible in leads V2 and V3. Left axis deviation is present (positive in I, negative in aVF and II).
report.impression: Sinus rhythm with Parkinsonian tremor artifact mimicking atrial flutter.
